Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to write a DuckDB SQL query that does the following Given table event_log with columns case_id activity timestamp We need to find cases that contain a specific direct sequence of three activities Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order in that exact order consecutively no other event from the same case in between For those cases further filter to only those where the time elapsed between the Create Order event and the Confirm Order event of that sequence is more than 5 days After identifying such cases exclude them from final result So final result returns all events from event_log except those that belong to these cases

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Logic 1 For each case order by timestamp We need to find the sequence pattern Since we need consecutive events we can use LEAD or LAG window functions to check three consecutive rows Or we can use self-joins with row numbering Lets define a subquery that assigns a row number to each event within each case ordered by timestamp Then we can look for rows where activity = Approve Purchase Requisition and the next row rn1 has activity = Create Order and the next rn2 has activity = Confirm Order And we need to compute the time difference between the Create Order and Confirm Order events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: WITH numbered AS ( SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS rn FROM event_log ), sequences AS ( SELECT n1.case_id n1.timestamp AS approve_ts n2.timestamp AS create_ts n3.timestamp AS confirm_ts FROM numbered n1 JOIN numbered n2 ON n1.case_id = n2.case_id AND n1.rn + 1 = n2.rn JOIN numbered n3 ON n1.case_id = n3.case_id AND n1.rn + 2 = n3.rn WHERE n1.activity = Approve Purchase Requisition AND n2.activity = Create Order AND n3.activity = Confirm Order ), excluded_cases AS ( SELECT DISTINCT case_id FROM sequences WHERE confirm_ts - create_ts > INTERVAL 5 days ) SELECT e. FROM event_log e WHERE e.case_id NOT IN (SELECT case_id FROM excluded_cases) ORDER BY e.case_id e.timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: need to ensure exactly more than 5 days The time difference between Create Order and Confirm Order events If timestamp is datetime confirm_ts - create_ts > INTERVAL 5 days or DATEDIFF day create_ts confirm_ts > 5 DuckDB supports both Ill go with confirm_ts - create_ts > INTERVAL 5 days Note The prompt says the time elapsed between the Create Order event and the Confirm Order event of that sequence is more than 5 days So if difference > 5 days we only need to exclude the case if any of those sequences meets the >5 day condition The above does that selects case_id from sequences where condition holds then excludes those case_ids

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Make sure to handle cases where the sequence might overlap For example could there be sequence Approve Create Confirm but maybe theres another Approve right after that But were looking for consecutive events with those exact activities in that order Our self-join on exact rn+1 rn+2 ensures they are strictly consecutive in the row numbering However there might be events with the exact same timestamp The ROW_NUMBER will arbitrarily assign order but if timestamp ties the order is nondeterministic A better approach might be to use LEAD Actually we need direct succession in terms of timestamp ordering If two events have identical timestamps the concept of direct succession is ambiguous But we assume timestamps are unique or the order is defined Ill stick with ROW_NUMBER as its typical

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Alternative Use LEAD window function twice WITH with_next AS ( SELECT LEAD(activity) OVER (PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS next_activity LEAD(activity 2) OVER (PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS next_next_activity LEAD(timestamp) OVER (PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS next_ts LEAD(timestamp 2) OVER (PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp) AS next_next_ts FROM event_log ), sequences AS ( SELECT DISTINCT case_id FROM with_next WHERE activity = Approve Purchase Requisition AND next_activity = Create Order AND next_next_activity = Confirm Order AND (next_next_ts - next_ts) > INTERVAL 5 days ) SELECT e. FROM event_log e WHERE e.case_id NOT IN (SELECT case_id FROM sequences) ORDER BY e.case_id e.timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This is cleaner and directly computes time between Create next_ts and Confirm next_next_ts The LEAD approach will handle ties in a stable way since its windowed and deterministic with ROW_NUMBERs inherent ordering but still if timestamps equal the order is arbitrary LEAD uses the same window ORDER BY timestamp so its the same as ROW_NUMBER Ill present the query with LEAD The prompt doesnt mention any tie-breaking so its fine

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to output the SQL query as the final answer Ill just provide the query with explanation The user asked Please ensure proper grouping ordering and filtering so that the result set consists solely of events from the cases that are not filtered out So Ill include ORDER BY case_id timestamp at the end Ill write the full SQL
